title: Adding OR & NOT to automations "Builder"
excerpt:
Hi Folks, 
I’m creating a mock PTO management system. I’ve found it reasonably repetitive to do specific tasks using the custom automations builder (for lack of a better word). I think AND is a handy bitwise operator to …

posts.author: aalnussairi (Ahmed Al-Nussairi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Folks,

I’m creating a mock PTO management system. I’ve found it reasonably repetitive to do specific tasks using the custom automations builder (for lack of a better word). I think AND is a handy bitwise operator to have when building automations. Still, I believe having the OR and NOT bitwise operators can improve user experience tremendously.

In my use case, I have two status columns, one for status and one for decision. It would be great if I can set up an automation that looks something like this:

When status (decision) changes to something (approved) OR When status (decision) changes to something (rejected), change status (status) to something (processed) AND move item to group (processed).

The workaround would be to create two separate automations, which is somewhat tedious and time-consuming.

Automation 1:

When status (decision) changes to something (approved), change status (status) to something (processed) AND move item to group (processed).

Automation 2:

When status (decision) changes to something (rejected), change status (status) to something (processed) AND move item to group (processed).

In the example above, you can see how things could get really time consuming for complex boards and automations. Having the OR bitwise operator would be great for such situations.

Another Important bitwise operator would be the NOT operator; if I can choose when the status is not something, I don’t have to repeat the same logic in cases where there are more than two potential statuses.

An example use case is where monday.com is used as a CRM to reach out to everyone who is not a customer at a specified date (Just an example):

When a Date arrives (next scheduled interaction) AND Status is NOT something (customer) then send an email (example email) to someone (email column).

The current workaround is:

Automation 1:

When a Date arrives (next scheduled interaction) AND Status is something (lead) then send an email (example email) to someone (email column).

Automation 2:

When a Date arrives (next scheduled interaction) AND Status is something (vendor) then send an email (example email) to someone (email column).

Automation 3:

When a Date arrives (next scheduled interaction) AND Status is something (partner) then send an email (example email) to someone (email column).

…and if there are more categories in that status, there would be more automations needed…

The other bitwise operators would make the automations platform incredibly flexible and powerful. Still, the NOT, OR and AND (lol) operators are the most important as they underpin all logic systems. In my opinion, this is a must-have for monday.com automations.

Kind Regards,

Ahmed

posts.author: ljohnson (Laura Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed on the “OR” option! I just set up 15 automations that could have been 3, if I had an “or.”

Biggest use case: I want the same action to be triggered by three status options - just need that “and” to be an “or.”

Alternate option: let me select multiple status options.

image837×259 10.2 KB

posts.author: Rohan-TAP (Rohan Bhatia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @Julia-monday.com, is this feature being considered for a future launch? It would really be a HUGE timesaver if we could use OR or NOT while creating custom automations. Can you please provide an update?

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: Upvote, especially given there is no way to organize your automations into groups/folders. It’s very challenging to have many automations and no OR or NOT forces you into a lot of automations.

posts.author: AndrewJVR (Andrew Jansen Van Rensburg)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely in agreement with this. I have automations that are failing because the “trigger” is the same, ie when a status changes to something, move it somewhere. It’s the ‘somewhere’ that is different based on the status chosen. About 15 automations when it should only be 1. Is there any feedback on this?

posts.author: JKwan0304 (Jasper Avery  Andrada)
posts.cookedHtml: I absolutely agree with this. I have a project board that contains 600 automations, if this feature is included, it would cut down the automation by 50% more or less.

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you keep 600 automations straight? I want to implement some routing on our board and I’m afraid it will take our ~80 automations and turn it into over 200. It’s already hard enough to find the automation that I’m trying to adjust, turn off, delete. It’s also painful to misclick and have it exit where you were scrolled and take you all the way to the top of the automations again.

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: How has there been no response to this from Monday? @Julia-monday.com This seems pretty basic.

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: Was using Airtable for something different and just checked…they have the ability to use OR within the automations…as well as grouping automations to better manage them.

posts.author: Joey (Joe McCarver)
posts.cookedHtml:
Seriously - how is this not a thing already? It’s SO fundamental to logic operations.

Originally I was looking to make a column non-editable based on the values of other columns; basically, if the dependent columns were not complete, the overall status could not be changed to complete. Again - such a basic/fundamental thing.

But I had to resort to using 15 automation rules to change it back to ‘Incomplete’ if any of the dependent columns were any of the possible values besides ‘Complete’.

Then I wanted to allow overrides for special circumstances - but I had to get a 3rd party app to update a ‘Modified By’ people column when the override column value is changed…

Come on guys…

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml:
When creating a new automation with the new automation builder (which is awesome btw), you can currently set the trigger and the action. It would be great if we could also specify some “conditions”.

For example:
Trigger: When status changes from something to something
Conditions: If date is before sometime
Action: Notify someone

Taking it a step further, it would be really awesome if we could have multiple conditions so that we could build a complex combination of conditions such as:
IF (date is before sometime OR date is after some other time) AND (checkbox is checked)

posts.author: RimDK (Rimmert)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also love this feature.

To expand on this, an AND operator in addition to IF and OR would also be great. That way an automation would be triggered of two (or more) conditions are met.

By the way, I would also love it if one trigger could be used to start more than one action in the automation builder.

posts.author: joshua.davison (Josh Davison)
posts.cookedHtml:
I thought I’d put an AND operator in my last example, but I hadn’t - I’ve edited to include that now.

Also love the idea of multiple actions in a single automation.

posts.author: roliver99 (Roger Oliver)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for requesting this feature enhancement. I completely agree. I see that the “AND” feature has been added for custom automations, which is very helpful. But, it would be extremely helpful if we could also do:

  • Ability to include an “OR” condition
  • Ability to include a “NOT” condition
  • Ability to nest conditions

posts.author: Hybrice (Matt Cornall)
posts.cookedHtml:
We also need this desperately, the current AND feature is very limited, only able to condition from status or assignee, which is useless if the automation was based on status anyway.

Example, we need to be able to:
IF status is X
AND custom field is Y
THEN do Z

posts.author: simon_blinno (Simon Blickenstorfer)
posts.cookedHtml: If logic is planned for Q4 2021 according this board from monday: https://view.monday.com/1092205827-f4c654f1e91a8e4e76eedbb64150c940?r=use1

posts.author: emcarr (Emilie Carr)
posts.cookedHtml: I need this feature too! Just for something as simple as creating recurring tasks that auto-push deadlines to the next month, six months, etc based on a status column.

posts.author: Adrien361 (Adrien)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m looking for the same thing with people and the creation of items on boards: when the status changes to something, and the person is (…), create an item on such board…

posts.author: simon_blinno (Simon Blickenstorfer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

If you want to do this now and can’t wait, you’ll have to do it with an external tool such as Integromat.

Here is an example for @emcarr. I have not tried it exactly, it may be that a format is not yet exactly right but theoretically you can rebuild it like that.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

Firstly, we sincerely apologise for the delay in responding to this thread! We are working on improving our responsiveness to the community, particularly our feature request process so appreciate your patience in advance!

On that note, if you haven’t already noticed, conditions in automations are now supported when building custom automations

Let me know if you have any additional comments or feedback.

posts.author: max (max)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bianca, it appears that “AND” conditions are available for custom automations, but nothing else. Is that the case, or am I missing how to add “OR” conditions? negative conditions?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @max,

My apologies, you’re correct Whilst AND is supported, we are yet to support OR / negative conditions. So that we can consolidate all votes and better prioritise this request, would you be happy to redirect your votes to the primary thread for this request (most voted) here: Adding OR & NOT to the custom automations "Builder".

This way, we can plan to close this thread so we have we have all the votes in one place!

Thank you

posts.author: Tapias87 (Marc)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there!
I agree would be great to have this update.
Kind Regards
Marc

posts.author: MichaelCrownSigns (Michael )
posts.cookedHtml: Do we have an update on this? The “or” is a fundamental way of cutting down automations and making operations much more smoother.

posts.author: cphu (Christine Phu)
posts.cookedHtml: +1, for example, Hubspot has a really great automations feature. With Monday, I have to create a new automation for every OR and it becomes hard to manage.

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: @cphu Monday is releasing the Workflow builder (to replace or in addition to automations, not sure which) soon and this looks like it has the ability to have several IF statements that will accomplish this. Check out this post: Our new workflow builder is coming and we are looking for 'Alpha' users!

posts.author: Curtis_F (Curtis Flick)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s crazy this isn’t already a feature and there have been little to no responses from Monday. Makes me reconsider signing up when my trial ends in one day. Do I need to do most of my integrations through zapier?

posts.author: StephCas (Stephanie Casertano)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree. I desperately need this and other “if” functions. I’m trying to work around this with just creating a million positive rules, but they are overlapping and causing conflicts.

posts.author: EMorton (Erik Morton)
posts.cookedHtml:
+1 from me. I continuously lament that I need to create far more automation recipes than seems necessary for my use cases, purely because I can’t have automation recipes say 'if status is this, OR if status is this, OR if status is this"

  • For example, I just created a board that needed 50 different automations that would set different status values based on a pair of other values (long story short: the other two columns essentially had numeric values, and the new status being set in the third column was a combination of the two)… but if I could have used ‘OR’ statements, that would have easily cut that down to only 9 recipes needed.

posts.author: jshubert (Jeremy Shubert)
posts.cookedHtml: When the Workflow Builder is released, I think this will be solved. I haven’t seen a timeline from Monday on this though: Our new workflow builder is coming and we are looking for 'Alpha' users!

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
After discussing the the community feedback with our team, I am excited to share that this feature is on our upcoming roadmap. With monday Workflows, you can already use If/Not options in your automations and the team is working on a new conditions component, that will support And, Or, Is, Is not, Equal, Not equal. Even more robust conditions coming in the near future! Stay tuned.

Keep sharing and voting for new ideas and learn more about how our feature request process works here.

posts.author: aalnussairi (Ahmed Al-Nussairi)
posts.cookedHtml: AMAZING! This is gonna be game changing

posts.author: mzona (Marisa Zona)
posts.cookedHtml: This is such a critical missing feature.

posts.author: JB007 (JulesWarner)
posts.cookedHtml: So this basic logical condition for the IF predicate, namely the OR logical condition, still isn’t part of Monday.com despite being raised and then committed to on the roadmap over 2 years ago or am I missing something?

posts.author: Adam (Adamski Piggins)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s a bit insane, isn’t it?

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml: Just wanted to provide an update here.The product team is working on this capability in monday workflows. With the new workflow builder you will be able to add “OR” and “NOT” to your automations. Our team is aiming to finalize this feature for the workflow builder. Stay tuned and make sure your notifications are turned on to get the latest updates.

posts.author: Krunchy (Guillaume Servonnat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good news !
I just wonder why this possibility would be only available on monday workflow, which is not complete at all compared to the actual automation builder especially regarding integrations, and also because it says Monday workflow might request additional payment later.
So I’m a bit confused…
Is is possible to clarify ?

posts.author: Leigh_73 (Leigh Skilton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, what timescales are we looking at here? (since this topic is 3 years old, and ive just started using Monday and have searched for this function)

To be clear, im looking for the OR functionality in Automations.

posts.author: michele-taccpas.com (Michele Mulvena)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need the OR functionality in Automations.

posts.author: jstokes (Joanne Stokes)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very helpful to set up situations for when an item is created and a status is ___ OR when a status is changed to ___. Currently we have to create two automations to capture both possibilities.

posts.author: ElectricEthen (Ethen Roth)
posts.cookedHtml: I just had to manually make 30 different automations because this feature does not exist yet. Would be VERY helpful!

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: While it’s still beta and doesn’t have many triggers, I think you should check out Workflows since they have the option to preform actions using If/Else

posts.author: mstepan (Marek Stepan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Mondy, what is the expected release date?

Thanks, Marek

posts.author: derekbosomworth (Derek)
posts.cookedHtml: Also needing this function for a customer. Is there a timeline for this function release?

posts.author: BillMillios (Bill Millios)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need OR, and NOT. Sigh. This is supremely frustrating.

posts.author: mstepan (Marek Stepan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Mondy, would you be so kind and share approx. ETA of the release date?

Thanks, Marek

posts.author: Sendhousestome (Sidney Blevins)
posts.cookedHtml: Came here for a solution, but found disappoint. @Monday, please add this feature.

posts.author: josbri (Josh)
posts.cookedHtml:
I understand that this topic was marked as “solved” but let’s be honest that it is not. The functionality that myself and I believe most everyone else is looking for is the ability to use OR/NOT in the AUTOMATIONS for a board, not a workflow outside of it.

It says “on the roadmap” and has been what looks like a frequent request for almost 3 years just by looking at this thread. Is there a plan to implement this in the near future? I can only speak for myself when I say this functionality is far more valuable to me than that of a cartoon llama webchat that I (and I am sure a others) will never use.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
The ability to add OR & NOT is now available in the monday workflows (enterprise & pro). The team does not currently have plans for it to be released within custom automations builder.

Want to learn more about the different features available on the new monday workflows?

  • Check out the workflows lesson on monday academy
  • Read this article in our knowledge base
  • Sign up for early access to monday workflows (enterprise only)
  • Watch @Maor community session

posts.author: Leigh_73 (Leigh Skilton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for letting us know @Julietteb . Can you be more specific where OR & NOT are in the workflow? as ive just had a look and cant see them mentioned specifically, nor in the resources listed.

Thank you.

posts.author: Kellie272 (Kellie)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would highly disagree that this issue is solved. Will the OR condition ever be added to automations? I don’t need a workflow, I just don’t want 300 automations that could easily be 30.

I’ll also add that they video shows nothing regarding the OR condition, it only showed the IF condition

posts.author: meghandurbin (Meghan Durbin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this Juliette, but I agree with Kellie even within workflows as shown in the lesson this doesn’t seem fixed.

I don’t see where I can find out if my company has an enterprise or pro account BUT seems like a simple tool that should be available across the board. To that point, the articles and lesson only refer to enterprise, not pro. Maybe we do have one of those accounts and it’s just not available to us yet. But I don’t want to ask for early access without knowing if we are enterprise or not. It might be helpful to let all of us know why this is such a challenge.

I absolutely love Monday and don’t have anywhere near the number of automations other users are referring to, and yet! This tool would be so helpful.

posts.author: AndrewJVR (Andrew Jansen Van Rensburg)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Meghan, you can find what account type you are in under the billing tab under Administration

posts.author: kasperhusted (Kasper Husted)
posts.cookedHtml:
When can we expect Workflows to be released to all Enterprise accounts?
I was told that this would have happened by the end of August, but it has yet to pop up on our account.

posts.author: AlichiaS (Ali Sawitoski)
posts.cookedHtml: Where is there any documentation for OR & NOT? None of the links you provided back in July actually reference that.

posts.author: meredithbradley (Meredith Bradley)
posts.cookedHtml: I have been using the new workflow builder and there is no option for OR or NOT. You only have the ability to do IF/THEN statements. Which, while also handy, is not needed nearly as much as OR or NOT. Can you please put this back on the roadmap?

posts.author: skmdc (SK)
posts.cookedHtml: It says the feature is “Released” but DOES NOT EXISTS in the Recipe creation logic as of Oct 23, 2024. When should we expect this? It has been 3 years since the feature was requested and is such a basic logical condition to have in any automation.

posts.author: meredithbradley (Meredith Bradley)
posts.cookedHtml: I was able to find that when they are referring to “OR” they don’t mean spelt out like a typical statement. In the Workflow they allow you to pick multiple options for status. So technically it follows as if it were OR.

posts.author: skmdc (SK)
posts.cookedHtml: How do you select multiple label values in the recipe? For example, When Status changes to … (I want red, yellow values); how do I select these from the dropdown?

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! We’ve heard your requests for OR and NOT conditions in the custom automation builder, and we want to let you know that this feature is planned! However, due to current prioritization, it will be released later in 2025.

In the meantime:

  • OR conditions are available for our 3 most popular conditions (if status is something, if item is in group, if person is someone).
  • NOT conditions can be achieved using “If/Else” conditions.

Both options are currently available in monday workflows (exclusive to Enterprise plans).

Thank you for your feedback - it’s invaluable in shaping our roadmap!
